title: Frontend Runtime Integration Readiness
purpose: Prepare the Zeptalytic Web Backend to be safely called by the React/Vite frontend during local development and future browser-based integration work.

context.summary: The backend now runs locally with uvicorn and exposes /docs and /openapi.json. The frontend is still static and will be integrated in a separate repo. This backend spec should add only the runtime and contract support needed for browser-based frontend integration.
context.repo_boundary: This spec is backend-only and should be run from Dev/Zeptalytic_Web_Backend.

global_guardrails: Do not redesign backend domain APIs.
Do not implement new frontend behavior in the backend repo.
Do not duplicate Pay commercial rules.
Do not expose sensitive auth/session/payment data.
Keep cookie-based auth as the current model.
Allow browser credentialed requests only for explicitly configured local/dev origins.
Generate as many focused spec items as the workstream context requires; do not cap specs at an arbitrary 3–4 implementation items.
Run python -m compileall app tests alembic before marking implementation items complete.
Run docker compose -f docker-compose.test.yml up --build --abort-on-container-exit --exit-code-from test before marking implementation items complete.

items.title: Add CORS Runtime Settings
items.description: Add settings for local/dev frontend origins and credentialed browser requests.
acceptance_criteria: Settings include configurable CORS allowed origins.
Settings include whether credentialed CORS requests are allowed.
Defaults support local Vite dev at http://localhost:5173 and http://127.0.0.1:5173.
No production wildcard credentialed CORS behavior is introduced.

items.title: Wire FastAPI CORS Middleware
items.description: Register FastAPI CORSMiddleware so the React/Vite frontend can call the backend with cookie credentials during local development.
acceptance_criteria: CORSMiddleware is registered in app/main.py before routers are included.
The configured origins are used rather than hard-coded only in middleware.
Credentialed requests are supported for configured origins.
Tests verify CORS preflight behavior for http://localhost:5173.

items.title: Verify Cookie-Based Auth Browser Contract
items.description: Add focused tests or documentation proving that auth endpoints support browser cookie-based session behavior for frontend integration.
acceptance_criteria: Login/signup behavior remains cookie-based.
Session cookie name matches settings.auth_session_cookie_name.
Documentation states that frontend requests must use credentials: include.
No localStorage/sessionStorage token model is introduced.

items.title: Document Frontend API Route Inventory
items.description: Generate or document the current route surface needed for the first frontend integration phases.
acceptance_criteria: A backend route inventory document exists under docs/architecture or equivalent.
The route inventory includes auth, dashboard, launcher, billing, profile/settings/address, rewards, support/status/announcements, and integrations routes where present.
The route inventory references /api/v1 as the current API prefix.
The document is safe to copy into or reference from the frontend repo.

items.title: Add OpenAPI Surface Regression Test
items.description: Add a regression test that verifies the core frontend-needed API paths are present in app.openapi().
acceptance_criteria: Test verifies /api/v1/auth/login is present.
Test verifies /api/v1/auth/session is present.
Test verifies /api/v1/dashboard/summary is present.
Test verifies /api/v1/launcher/products is present.
Test verifies core billing paths are present.
The test does not require live Pay or Discord services.

items.title: Verify Backend Frontend-Readiness Baseline
items.description: Run required backend verification commands after CORS and route contract readiness are complete.
acceptance_criteria: python -m compileall app tests alembic passes.
docker compose -f docker-compose.test.yml up --build --abort-on-container-exit --exit-code-from test passes.
progress/progress.txt is updated at EOF.
No unrelated frontend files are touched from the backend repo.
